naming_registers: post-Earth creole drift — given names that are recognizable Earth roots fused or vowel-shifted across generations (Yuko-Ade, Marisol-7, Jaq, Nnedi-Vance)
colony-founder commemorative names — children named for the original founder, terrain feature, or first ship to make landfall
corporate-bloc surnames — surname is the corp or the corp-issued employee block, given name is normal (Adaeze HelionCorp, Ramsey Vex-Dynam)
second-wave colonial Spanglish-Tagalog drift — given names pooling Iberian, Filipino, and Andean roots (Esperanza, Datu, Mateo, Kakay)
outer-rim Slavic-Mongolian descent — names from Kazakh, Mongolian, Russian, Ukrainian families that emigrated three generations back
machine-given designations — for synthetics: model-and-version style, sometimes adopted as a name (M-7 'Em', Charon-Rev3, Sister Whittle of Line 4)
ship-clan names among Belters and Drift-folk — given name plus the ship you crew on plus a generational tag (Iza of the Cormorant, fourth-burn)
old-Earth holdouts — families that deliberately preserve a single ethnic naming tradition as identity
underworld and cantina nicknames — single-word descriptors (Patch, Slim, the Fixer, Hollowtooth) more known than legal identity
academy and explorer-corps callsigns — military-style honorifics carried into civilian life (Captain Ortega, Probe-Lead Ushiro, Doc Wen)
district_flavors: habitation-ring deck levels — gravity falls off the higher you live, and so does respectability
cargo concourse and bonded-warehouse strip where every shipment is half-legal
ship-breaker yard around a derelict megastructure with families living inside the cooling hulks
diplomatic envoy berths — embassy ships docked at a neutral station, each flying its own atmosphere mix
frontier ice-mining settlement under a pressure dome, with one bar, one chapel, one airlock out
salvage-broker bazaar in a station's lower decks — alien artifacts of dubious provenance changing hands cash-only
academy and university orbital — research labs, low-G dormitories, and corporate recruiters
corporate company-town — single employer, scrip wages, branded everything, and a charter that's almost legal
drift-folk floating community of lashed-together ships, no station affiliation, internal politics by clan
